title: Sony deletes more movies from the accounts of people who ‘bought’ them
titleZh: 索尼从“购买”了这些影片的用户账户中删除更多电影
author: nekusar
relevanceReason: 该帖子讨论了索尼从PlayStation Store用户账户中删除已购买电影和电视节目，涉及数字版权、消费者权利和数字产品所有权问题，与科技产品和数字服务相关。
summaryRaw: In all of our discussions about how the digital revolution has created a system in which people don’t actually own the things they think they’re buying, I get particularly frustrated by the lack of change in it all. We’ve spilled much ink complaining that this clearly anti-consumer practice needs to be done away with, where an unsuspecting public thinks they’re buying “a thing” only to learn months or years later that “the thing” they bought was actually a license to use/view/listen to another “thing”, and that license exists at the pleasure of the company that collected the money for it. And if you want to see the lack of change or action really honed in upon, let’s take a look at Sony’s PlayStation Store. In 2022, due to “evolving licensing agreements” with distributor StudioCanal, German and Austrian users had hundreds of movies disappear from their PS accounts, long after buying them through Sony. Then in 2023, it happened again in America, specifically when Sony ended its licensing agreement with Discovery after the Warner Bros. merger, which, of course, has since been bought by Paramount Skydance. That resulted in customers having hundreds and hundreds of episodes of TV shows deleted from their accounts. Nowhere in any of this were there refunds, of course. No recompense at all, actually. Just a thing you thought you’d bought taken away from you by the very people you thought you bought it from. And now it’s happening again. Due to another licensing agreement fallout with StudioCanal, hundreds of movies and TV shows are being ripped from the accounts of PS Store customers, and there appears to be fuck all that they can do about it.

This news was brought to people’s attention by X user somatyk, who posted the notification they had received from PlayStation this week. Along with the unapologetic news that the purchased movies would be deleted from their account on September 1, the message concluded with, “Click here for a full list of affected titles that will no longer be supported. Thank you.” The same warning is now reproduced in full on the PlayStation website, along with the list of 551 films and TV series that are being pulled from people’s libraries.

As Kotaku notes later in their post, part of what is striking in all of this is the sheer mundanity of the announcement. Because there have been no consequences, or any action at all from the public or government, Sony treats this all as if it’s perfectly normal and no big deal. You can tell me all you want about how the Ts and Cs in these purchases do in fact note that the nature of the purchase is a temporary licensing of the content for an undetermined time period… but I can promise you that the public in general doesn’t understand that. They think they’re buying a thing, not a license. And that’s because of the purposeful obfuscation of that fact. Sony damned well knows that the vast majority of people don’t read those Ts and Cs. It knows that the public largely doesn’t understand how these backend licensing agreements with distributors work, or that they even exist. And Sony isn’t exactly putting out a big blinking sign on its store pages informing the public of all of this. Instead, the company is only too happy to collect money from a public that is being purposefully kept ignorant of what they’re buying.

Of course, when you scroll past the endless EULAs when you first use your PlayStation, and click “Agree” the first time you load the store, you’re unwittingly agreeing that nothing you buy is really truly bought, and that it can be taken away from you at any point, and there’s nothing you can do. The same is true of your games.
summaryZh: 索尼再次从PlayStation Store用户账户中删除数百部已购买的电影和电视节目，原因是与StudioCanal的授权协议终止。
用户实际上购买的是内容的使用许可而非所有权，这些许可可以在公司决定时随时被撤销，且没有退款。
此类事件反映了数字革命中消费者对所购数字产品缺乏真正所有权的问题，且公众对此认知不足。
highlightsZh: 索尼因授权协议变动多次删除用户已购内容，影响德国、奥地利及美国用户。
用户购买的是临时使用许可而非内容所有权，许可可随时被撤销且无补偿。
索尼通过复杂的条款和条件故意模糊消费者对所购内容性质的理解。
此类行为未受到公众或政府的有效制约，索尼将其视为正常操作。
用户在初次使用PlayStation时同意的EULA中已默认接受这些条款。

comments.author: Nevermark
comments.textRawHtml:
Revocation should come with full refunds.

That would:

1. Balance the revocation economically, for both parties, while leaving the decision to the "seller".

2. The trade becomes the time-value of money vs. the time value of access. Inherently fair. The seller nets interest, and the inflation drop on the original price. What an accountant would come up with, yet automatic.

3. Provide users the remunerative recourse for "resuming" their "perpetual" license with another provider.

4. Motivate the avoidance of revocations, as who wants to have anti-sales.

Maybe there are good reasons for revocations. Fine, but purchasers should not "Get" randomly screwed, while the seller who had control of their sourcing arrangements loses nothing.

"Get" instead of "Buy" does not address the problem. If "Get" requires the user to gamble, it should be "Gamble". "Get for five years" or "Get for 5 viewings" would be ok. But "Get" without a clear definition is inherently misleading. Another dark pattern.

(Also: By law, contracts must be something given for something taken. A one-sided uncompensated nullification-at-will option is a sneaky way around that. Companies that nullify without compensation, or less than full price where the licensing agreement made no refund amount declaration, should be required to return customer money with interest to reflect the bad faith contract. IANAL, just a believer in justice, especially where simple accounting provides answers.)
comments.textZhHtml:
撤销应全额退款。

这样：

1.在经济上平衡双方的撤销，同时将决定权留给“卖方”。

2.交易变成了货币的时间价值与访问的时间价值。本质上是公平的。卖方扣除利息，通货膨胀率在原价基础上下降。会计师会自动想出什么。

3。为用户提供有偿资源，让他们可以通过其他提供商“恢复”其“永久”许可证。

4.激励避免撤销，就像谁想要反销售一样。

也许撤销有充分的理由。好吧，但买家不应该随意“获取”，而控制其采购安排的卖家则不会损失任何东西。

“获取”而不是“购买”并不能解决问题。如果“获取”要求用户赌博，则应该是“赌博”。 “获得五年”或“获得五次观看”就可以了。但没有明确定义的“获取”本质上是一种误导。另一个黑暗模式。

（另外：根据法律，合同必须是有所得必有所获。片面无偿的随意无效选项是一种偷偷摸摸的解决办法。在没有补偿的情况下无效的公司，或者在许可协议没有声明退款金额的情况下低于全价的公司，应该被要求返还客户资金和利息，以反映恶意合同。IANAL，只是正义的信徒，特别是在简单的会计提供答案的情况下。）

children.author: Reason077
children.textRawHtml:
> ”Revocation should come with full refunds.”

Of course it should. If Sony are not doing that then it sounds like grounds for a class action?

I remember when Google shut down Stadia. To their credit, they automatically refunded everything I’d ever purchased on the platform. Nice little windfall.
children.textZhHtml:
> “撤销应全额退款。”

当然应该。如果索尼不这样做，那么这听起来像是集体诉讼的理由？

我记得谷歌关闭 Stadia 时。值得赞扬的是，他们自动退还了我在该平台上购买的所有商品。不错的小意外之财。

children.author: apparent
children.textRawHtml: The lawsuit would have to prove that Sony didn't abide by it's T&Cs, which it seems like they may well have. The issue then is whether it's OK to have T&Cs that seemingly conflict with the "buy" characterization that customers are presented with.
children.textZhHtml: 诉讼必须证明索尼没有遵守其条款和条件，而他们似乎很可能遵守了条款和条件。接下来的问题是，是否可以制定看似与向客户呈现的“购买”特征相冲突的条款和条件。

children.author: Reason077
children.textRawHtml: Just because something is written in T&Cs doesn’t make it okay. Consumer laws have precedence over T&Cs.
children.textZhHtml: 条款与条件里写了的东西不代表就是合理的。消费者保护法优先于条款与条件。

children.author: apparent
children.textRawHtml: Consumer protection laws can prevail over terms and conditions, it's true. Are there any consumer protection laws that disallow terms and conditions like the ones Sony used?
children.textZhHtml: 消费者保护法确实可以优先于条款与条件，这没错。但是，有没有哪条消费者保护法禁止使用索尼所用的那种条款与条件呢？

children.author: cogman10
children.textRawHtml:
I believe the FTC could probably do something about it if they cared.

This seems to be something that the "Unfair or Deceptive Acts or Practices" charter of the FTC could address.

The three prongs of when it applies are

- Causes or is likely to cause substantial injury to consumers

- Cannot be reasonably avoided by consumers

- Is not outweighed by countervailing benefits to consumers or to competition

It seems like stealing movies users purchased thinking they owned them checks all 3 boxes.
children.textZhHtml:
我认为，如果联邦贸易委员会在意的话，他们大概能对此采取一些行动。

这似乎属于联邦贸易委员会“不公平或欺骗性行为或做法”章程可以解决的范畴。

其适用的三个条件是：

- 造成或可能造成消费者重大损害

- 消费者无法合理避免

- 其对消费者或竞争带来的抵消性利益不足以弥补损害

窃取用户以为已经买断的电影，这似乎完全符合这三个条件。

children.author: apparent
children.textRawHtml: Generally agree, if Sony is deleting things from user-owned devices. If they're simply not making them available for later download, it's trickier. The case against Sony would be stronger if customers could not move downloaded content from their current devices.
children.textZhHtml: 大体上同意，前提是索尼在从用户拥有的设备上删除内容。如果他们只是不再提供后续下载，情况就更复杂了。如果客户无法将已下载的内容从当前设备转移走，那么针对索尼的指控就会更有说服力。

children.author: jjav
children.textRawHtml: They should I agree, but consumer protection has been decimated in the US. Also the CFPB has effectively been dismantled.  All is fair game as long as the oligarchs get a few more millions they don't need.
children.textZhHtml: 我同意理应如此，但美国的消费者保护机制已遭到严重破坏。此外，消费者金融保护局实际上也已被瓦解。只要寡头们能多捞几百万他们根本不需要的钱，什么手段都使得出来。

children.author: reilly3000
children.textRawHtml: I strongly feel that if I click on a button that says “Buy” I am making a permanent purchase. The verb should be called “License” or “Lease” to reflect the nature of the transaction or it is inherently deceptive and should be illegal.
children.textZhHtml: 我强烈认为，如果我点击一个写着“购买”的按钮，就意味着我在进行永久性购买。该动词应改为“授权”或“租赁”，以反映交易的真实性质，否则这本质上就是一种欺骗行为，理应被定为非法。

children.author: worthless-trash
children.textRawHtml: Pretty sure the  ACCC (Australian Competition and Consumer Commission)  is going to have a field day with this.
children.textZhHtml: 我敢肯定，ACCC（澳大利亚竞争与消费者委员会）肯定会借此大做文章。

children.author: soraminazuki
children.textRawHtml: Those T&Cs contain clauses that allow companies to 1. arbitrarily change its terms whenever they want and 2. forbid consumers from suing them. They make a complete mockery of justice, and they might as well just write "haha, screw you" to save all of us time.
children.textZhHtml: 那些条款与条件中包含允许公司1. 随心所欲地任意更改条款，以及2. 禁止消费者起诉他们的条款。这完全是在把正义当儿戏，他们干脆直接写上“哈哈，去你的吧”，还能省大家点时间。

children.author: scoofy
children.textRawHtml: It's very obviously false advertising. You can't say "free flight to europe" and then in the fine print say "no free flight to europe." It's just blatant false advertising to have a "rent" next to a "buy" button you click to acquire what is effectively a long term rental.
children.textZhHtml: 这明显是虚假广告。你不能打着“免费飞往欧洲”的旗号，然后在细则里写“没有免费飞往欧洲”。在“购买”按钮旁边放一个“租赁”按钮，让用户点击后得到的实际上只是长期租赁权，这纯粹是明目张胆的虚假广告。

children.author: vitally3643
children.textRawHtml:
Incorrect. A lawsuit would have to prove Sony didn't abide by law, or that their actions are harmful or egregious enough to justify a new law.

T&C are not laws, and aren't even legally binding in most situations. They exist purely as an ass-covering maneuver and self-justification for customer abuse.
children.textZhHtml:
不对。诉讼必须证明索尼没有遵守法律，或者其行为具有足够的危害性或恶劣程度，从而有理由制定新法律。

条款与条件不是法律，在大多数情况下甚至不具备法律约束力。它们的存在纯粹是一种推卸责任的手段，也是为侵害消费者权益进行自我辩护的借口。

children.author: semiquaver
children.textRawHtml:
Terms and Conditions are contracts and have repeatedly been affirmed as such by the courts.

True, illegal contract provisions are not enforceable but there’s not actually an allegation that anything illegal has taken place.

It sounds like you are describing the world as you with it existed rather than as it actually does.
children.textZhHtml:
条款与条件是合同，法院已多次确认这一点。

诚然，非法的合同条款不具有强制执行力，但实际上并没有人指控发生了任何非法行为。

听起来你描述的是你希望存在的世界，而不是它实际的样子。

children.author: StilesCrisis
children.textRawHtml: They even refunded the controller and Chromecast Ultra, which still work to this day (just not to play Stadia).
children.textZhHtml: 他们甚至对控制器和 Chromecast Ultra 进行了退款，这些设备至今仍然可以使用（只是不能玩 Stadia）。

children.author: rlpb
children.textRawHtml:
> Revocation should come with full refunds.

That's not enough. If I purchase something, I expect to rely on being able to keep it. Its value to me could well be much higher than just the purchase price.

Revocation should come with consequential damages together with the cost of recovering them.
children.textZhHtml:
> 撤销应全额退款。

这还不够。如果我购买某样东西，我希望能够保留它。它对我来说的价值很可能远远高于购买价格。

撤销应该带来间接损失以及恢复损失的成本。

children.author: protimewaster
children.textRawHtml:
I agree. I honestly think it's strange that it's often viewed as "good enough" to get a refund.

If I paid $5 for a movie, I did that because I want the movie more than the $5. If I didn't, I would've kept the $5. You giving me $5 and taking the movie away is just replacing the thing I value more with the thing I value less.

The other issue is that, if I paid $5 for a movie 10 years ago, that's now more than $5 due to inflation. If you give me $5 back, you're short.
children.textZhHtml:
我同意。老实说，我认为这很奇怪，因为它经常被视为“足够好”才能获得退款。

如果我为一部电影支付 5 美元，我会这样做，因为我想要的电影不仅仅是 5 美元。如果我不这样做，我就会保留这 5 美元。你给我 5 美元，然后把电影拿走，这只是用我更不看重的东西替换了我更看重的东西。

另一个问题是，如果我 10 年前为一部电影支付 5 美元，那么现在由于通货膨胀，这将超过 5 美元。如果你还给我 5 美元，你就亏空了。

children.author: vitally3643
children.textRawHtml: It should be refunded with interest.

children.author: dev_l1x_be
children.textRawHtml: Also they would need to adjust the refund it with inflation.
children.textZhHtml: 他们还需要根据通货膨胀调整退款。
